demo_recommendations.name: Pipeline-math++ formal research proof packet
demo_recommendations.success_metric: one public theorem or bounded identity packet with source, solver, verifier, formalization, and Crucible verdicts

demo_recommendations.name: Agent observability-to-action-receipt proof packet
demo_recommendations.success_metric: one agent run with OTel-style trace refs, source refs, admission decision, stop reason, verification, and compensation pointer

demo_recommendations.name: BuildLang/color/rendering measurement proof kit
demo_recommendations.success_metric: one color transform or render measurement with source hash, runtime receipt, numeric threshold, visual artifact, and Crucible gate

forum_route_observation.finding: A broad cross-domain pass spanning AI4Science, compiler systems, rendering, market strategy, and proof packets did not cleanly route to one Forum lane; record as vocabulary and lane-composition gap.

megatool_nodes.market_facing_product: Research intake and source provenance layer
Shared receipt room and product shell
Inspectable evidence capture for demos and web research

non_promotion_statement: Pass 0063 is a market and architecture planning artifact. It does not prove product-market fit, scientific novelty, unique competitive absence, new physics, or buyer adoption.

opportunity_rows.buyer: math labs, theoretical CS groups, AI reasoning teams
opportunity_rows.category: Formal math and theoretical CS proof discovery
opportunity_rows.market_need: Frontier proof work needs source provenance, conjecture state, prover-verifier traces, formalization status, and human review in one packet.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: Telos can win by packaging a claim-to-proof object that connects source intake, workspace state, model attempts, Lean or checker status, Crucible verdicts, and publication artifacts.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: Pipeline-math++ proof packet with source anchors, solver attempts, verifier critiques, Lean status, negative cases, and Crucible claims.

opportunity_rows.buyer: research labs, industrial R&D, AI4Science teams
opportunity_rows.market_need: AI4Science platforms automate fragments of research, but buyers still need accountable provenance from literature to hypothesis to experiment to verdict.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: Telos can differentiate as the proof layer beneath AI scientist workflows rather than another closed research assistant.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: AI4Science claim packet joining literature receipts, hypothesis graph, experiment command, result digest, failed checks, and reviewer notes.

opportunity_rows.buyer: quantum software teams, HPC labs, accelerator platform groups
opportunity_rows.category: Quantum, hybrid HPC, and accelerator algorithms
opportunity_rows.market_need: Hybrid CPU/GPU/QPU work needs reproducible kernel, compiler, backend, simulator, hardware, and measurement receipts.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc plus Telos receipts can make hybrid scientific kernels auditable across compilation and execution boundaries.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: Hybrid kernel proof packet with source hash, compiler flags, backend target, simulator or hardware class, numeric result, invariant check, and Crucible verdict.

opportunity_rows.buyer: biology labs, biotech R&D, translational research teams
opportunity_rows.category: Biology, protein modeling, and drug discovery
opportunity_rows.market_need: Biology tools produce strong predictions and research assistance, but wet-lab, model, source, and review evidence often live in separate systems.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: Telos can supply preclinical claim packets that do not claim lab truth, but bind model prediction, source evidence, assumptions, and validation status.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: Protein-claim proof packet with literature receipts, model output hash, structure source, uncertainty label, wet-lab status, and explicit non-clinical boundary.

opportunity_rows.buyer: materials labs, climate tech teams, energy R&D organizations
opportunity_rows.market_need: Materials and climate work depends on data provenance, simulation assumptions, units, model versions, and reproducibility across tools.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: Telos can act as a receipt bus joining public datasets, simulation kernels, model forecasts, and verification gates.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: Materials candidate packet with dataset source, query digest, model/simulation command, units, uncertainty, falsifiers, and measurement plan.

opportunity_rows.buyer: scientific computing teams, quant teams, finance modeling, security engineering
opportunity_rows.category: Scientific compiler/runtime and accountable language layer
opportunity_rows.market_need: Scientific languages and compiler stacks optimize speed and expressiveness, but usually leave proof packets, receipts, and external verdicts outside the language runtime.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc can own accountable scientific computing by making source, compiler, runtime, measurement, and verifier receipts native.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: BuildLang runtime proof kit for a PDE, color transform, or quant kernel with compiler receipt, numeric invariant, failure fixture, and Crucible verdict.

opportunity_rows.buyer: AI platform teams, regulated automation teams, internal developer platforms
opportunity_rows.category: AI infrastructure, observability, and accountable agent actions
opportunity_rows.market_need: Observability and ML lifecycle tools capture traces, metrics, runs, and models, but action admission, authority, workspace state, and verifier verdicts are rarely bound as one portable object.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: Telos can be the accountable execution layer above observability, producing action proof packets for high-stakes workflows.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: Agent action packet with source refs, tool authority, command/material digest, trace refs, stop reason, verification verdict, and compensation pointer.

opportunity_rows.buyer: VFX teams, display QA teams, imaging labs, visual AI dataset reviewers
opportunity_rows.category: Color, rendering, and calibration proof kits
opportunity_rows.market_need: Color pipelines have standards and calibration tools, but measured outputs, transform code, display state, and verifier receipts are not usually portable claim objects.
opportunity_rows.primary_wedge_hypothesis: Hypothesis: Telos plus Build Color can make color-critical outputs reviewable as proof packets instead of screenshots or tool-native reports alone.
opportunity_rows.proof_demo: BuildLang/color measurement kit with transform source, ICC/LUT refs, display target, delta metrics, artifact hashes, and Crucible measurement gate.

source_anchors.label: Microsoft Discovery
source_anchors.summary: Enterprise agentic AI platform for scientific research, R&D, and advanced scientific computing.

source_anchors.label: FutureHouse
source_anchors.summary: Non-profit building AI agents to automate research in biology and other complex sciences.

source_anchors.label: Sakana AI Scientist
source_anchors.summary: System positioned as automating the research lifecycle from ideas through experiments and manuscript writing.

source_anchors.label: pipeline-math
source_anchors.summary: Public prover-verifier style math pipeline and proof-discovery repository.

source_anchors.label: OpenAI unit-distance result
source_anchors.summary: OpenAI describes a model-discovered discrete geometry construction and companion proof material.

source_anchors.label: LeanDojo
source_anchors.summary: Toolkit for training, evaluating, and deploying AI-assisted theorem provers for Lean 4.

source_anchors.label: NVIDIA CUDA-Q
source_anchors.summary: Open-source quantum development platform spanning CPU, GPU, and QPU resources.

source_anchors.label: Google DeepMind AlphaFold
source_anchors.summary: AlphaFold Server gives non-commercial researchers access to AlphaFold 3 structure prediction capabilities.

source_anchors.label: Materials Project API
source_anchors.summary: Open materials data access and API for computed information on known and predicted materials.

source_anchors.label: MLflow Model Registry
source_anchors.summary: Centralized model store with lineage, versioning, aliases, metadata tagging, and annotation.

source_anchors.label: OpenTelemetry
source_anchors.summary: Vendor-neutral framework for telemetry such as traces, metrics, and logs.

source_anchors.label: Julia
source_anchors.summary: Fast, dynamic, open-source language with a scientific and data ecosystem.

source_anchors.label: Mojo
source_anchors.summary: Language vision for targeting CPUs, GPUs, and accelerators with Python-like syntax and systems capabilities.

source_anchors.label: OpenXLA
source_anchors.summary: Open ecosystem for performant, portable, extensible ML infrastructure components.

source_anchors.label: Triton
source_anchors.summary: Language and compiler for parallel programming on modern GPU hardware.

source_anchors.label: OpenColorIO
source_anchors.summary: Color management solution for motion picture production, VFX, and computer animation.

source_anchors.label: ACES documentation
source_anchors.summary: Industry standard for managing color and digital files through media production lifecycles.

source_anchors.label: Calman
source_anchors.summary: Calibration and validation software with professional display workflows and reports.

source_anchors.label: ColourSpace
source_anchors.summary: 3D LUT display calibration and color-management software for color-critical workflows.
